query: What Dominion Energy commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge in Virginia?
final_answer:
# Dominion Energy Virginia: Highest Commercial Demand Charge

Based on the OpenEI Utility Rate Database (IURDB) and Dominion Energy's official tariff filings with the Virginia State Corporation Commission, the commercial service class that pays the **highest demand charge** is:

## **Schedule GS-3 — Large General Service (Secondary Voltage)**

### Demand Charge Breakdown (effective 01/01/2026)

| Component | Off-Peak ($/kW) | On-Peak ($/kW) |
|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| Base Distribution Demand | $3.636 | $14.569 |
| Rider Adjustments* | $10.39 | $10.39 |
| **Total Demand Charge** | **$14.03/kW** | **$24.96/kW** |

\*Adjustments include: Biomass Conversions, Brunswick County Power Station, Clean Energy Projects & Power Purchase Agreements, Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind, Environmental Projects, Grid Transformation, Greensville Power Station, Rural Broadband Pilot Projects, Surry & North Anna Nuclear Life Extension, Transmission, and US Solar Projects riders.

**Source:** [OpenEI IURDB — GS-3 Rate Record](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/6997d57a8f47e2933e0d6f4c); [Dominion Energy Schedule GS-3 PDF](https://cdn-dominionenergy-prd-001.azureedge.net/-/media/content/rates-and-tariffs/pdfs/virginia/large-business/schedule-gs3.pdf)

---

### Comparison Across All Active Dominion Energy Virginia Commercial Schedules

| Schedule | Service Class | Size | Highest Demand Charge ($/kW) |
|----------|--------------|------|------------------------------|
| **GS-3** | **Large General Service — Secondary** | **≥500 kW** | **$24.96 (on-peak)** |
| GS-4 | Large General Service — Primary | ≥500 kW | $23.59 (on-peak, first 5,000 kW) |
| GS-2T | Intermediate General Service TOU | 30–500 kW | $16.19 (summer on-peak) |
| GS-2 Demand | Intermediate General Service | 30–500 kW | $12.79 (winter) |
| Schedule 10 (Sec.) | Large General Service | ≥500 kW | $6.51 (flat) |
| Schedule 10 (Pri.) | Large General Service | ≥500 kW | $6.19 (flat) |
| 5P | Church & Synagogue TOU | — | $9.73 |
| GS-1 | Small General Service | <30 kW | $0 (no demand charge) |
| GS-2 Non-Demand | Intermediate General Service | 30–500 kW | $0 (energy-only) |

**Sources:** [OpenEI IURDB — Virginia Electric & Power Co (EIA ID 19876)](https://apps.openei.org/USURDB/?page=3&utilRateFinder=Dominion+Virginia+Power); [Dominion Energy Virginia Business Rates](https://www.dominionenergy.com/virginia/rates-and-tariffs/business-rates); [Dominion GS-2T Tariff PDF](https://www.dominionenergy.com/-/media/content/rates-and-tariffs/pdfs/virginia/large-business/schedule-gs2t.pdf)

---

### Key Takeaway

**Schedule GS-3** customers — large commercial/industrial facilities served at **secondary voltage** with peak demands of **500 kW or more** — pay the highest demand charge at **$24.96 per kW** during on-peak hours (summer: 10 a.m.–10 p.m. weekdays, June–September). This is notably higher than the GS-4 (primary voltage) rate of $23.59/kW, reflecting the additional distribution infrastructure costs associated with serving large loads at secondary voltage rather than primary voltage.

# Dominion Energy Virginia: Highest Commercial Demand Charge

Based on the OpenEI Utility Rate Database (IURDB) and Dominion Energy's official tariff filings with the Virginia State Corporation Commission, the commercial service class that pays the **highest demand charge** is:

## **Schedule GS-3 — Large General Service (Secondary Voltage)**

### Demand Charge Breakdown (effective 01/01/2026)

| Component | Off-Peak ($/kW) | On-Peak ($/kW) |

| **Total Demand Charge** | **$14.03/kW** | **$24.96/kW** |

\*Adjustments include: Biomass Conversions, Brunswick County Power Station, Clean Energy Projects & Power Purchase Agreements, Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind, Environmental Projects, Grid Transformation, Greensville Power Station, Rural Broadband Pilot Projects, Surry & North Anna Nuclear Life Extension, Transmission, and US Solar Projects riders.

This is notably higher than the GS-4 (primary voltage) rate of $23.59/kW, reflecting the additional distribution infrastructure costs associated with serving large loads at secondary voltage rather than primary voltage.
steps.reasoning:
Let me analyze the data I've collected to determine which Dominion Energy Virginia commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge.

From the OpenEI IURDB data for Virginia Electric & Power Co (EIA ID 19876), I can see the following active commercial rate schedules in Virginia:

1. **GS-1 (Small General Service)** - Single Phase and Three Phase
   - No explicit demand charge (flat demand structure, no demand rate structure)
   - These are for customers under 30 kW

2. **GS-2 Demand** (Intermediate General Service, 30-500 kW)
   - Flat demand structure (applies Oct-May and Jun-Sep differently):
     - Winter (Oct-May): $8.737/kW + $4.048/kW adjustments = **$12.785/kW**
     - Summer (Jun-Sep): $7.522/kW + $4.048/kW adjustments = **$11.57/kW**

3. **GS-2 Non-Demand** (Intermediate General Service, 30-500 kW)
   - No demand charge (flat demand structure not present, energy-only)

4. **GS-2T Time-of-Use** (Intermediate General Service, 30-500 kW)
   - Demand rate structure with 3 tiers:
     - Tier 1: $4.963/kW + $4.39/kW adjustments = **$9.353/kW** (distribution demand)
     - Tier 2: $11.802/kW + $4.39/kW adjustments = **$16.192/kW** (on-peak summer)
     - Tier 3: $9.491/kW + $4.39/kW adjustments = **$13.881/kW** (on-peak winter or shoulder)

   Wait, let me re-read the GS-2T tariff document more carefully:
   - Distribution Demand Charge: $4.963/kW
   - Generation Demand Charge:
     - Summer (Jun-Sep): $4.526/kW (on-peak)
     - Winter (Oct-May): $2.215/kW (on-peak)
   - Transmission Demand Charge: $2.313/kW (on-peak)
   
   So total summer on-peak demand = $4.963 + $4.526 + $2.313 = $11.802/kW (before riders/adjustments)
   Total winter on-peak demand = $4.963 + $2.215 + $2.313 = $9.491/kW (before riders/adjustments)

   With adjustments of $4.39/kW:
   - Summer: $11.802 + $4.39 = **$16.192/kW**
   - Winter: $9.491 + $4.39 = **$13.881/kW**

5. **GS-3 (Large General Service Secondary Voltage)** (≥500 kW)
   - Demand rate structure with 2 tiers:
     - Tier 1 (off-peak): $3.636/kW + $10.39/kW adjustments = **$14.026/kW**
     - Tier 2 (on-peak): $14.569/kW + $10.39/kW adjustments = **$24.959/kW**

6. **GS-4 (Large General Service Primary Voltage)** (≥500 kW)
   - Demand rate structure with 2 tiers, each with sub-tiers:
     - Tier 1 (off-peak): 
       - First 5,000 kW: $3.275/kW + $0.268/kW = $3.543/kW
       - Over 5,000 kW: $2.634/kW + $0.268/kW = $2.902/kW
     - Tier 2 (on-peak):
       - First 5,000 kW: $14.524/kW + $9.068/kW = **$23.592/kW**
       - Over 5,000 kW: $13.883/kW + $9.068/kW = **$22.951/kW**

   Wait, let me re-read the GS-4 data more carefully:
   - demandratestructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":3.275,"adj":0.268},{"rate":2.634,"adj":0.268}],[{"max":5000,"rate":14.524,"adj":9.068},{"rate":13.883,"adj":9.068}]]
   
   So:
   - Off-peak: First 5,000 kW = $3.275 + $0.268 = $3.543/kW; Over 5,000 kW = $2.634 + $0.268 = $2.902/kW
   - On-peak: First 5,000 kW = $14.524 + $9.068 = $23.592/kW; Over 5,000 kW = $13.883 + $9.068 = $22.951/kW

   Plus there's a flat demand structure:
   - flatdemandstructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":3.316,"adj":3.189},{"rate":2.076,"adj":3.469}]]
   
   Wait, that's for Schedule 10, not GS-4. Let me re-check.

   Actually, looking at the data again:
   - GS-4 entry (69e99cf96746522c70079990):
     - demandratestructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":3.275,"adj":0.268},{"rate":2.634,"adj":0.268}],[{"max":5000,"rate":14.524,"adj":9.068},{"rate":13.883,"adj":9.068}]]
     - No flat demand structure listed for GS-4

   Wait, actually looking more carefully at the GS-4 entry, it does have a flatdemandstructure:
   - flatdemandstructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":3.316,"adj":3.189},{"rate":2.076,"adj":3.469}]]
   
   Hmm, but that seems to be from the Schedule 10 entry. Let me re-check.

   Actually, looking at the data more carefully:

   Entry 69e99cf96746522c70079990 is GS-4 (Large General Service Primary Voltage):
   - demandratestructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":3.275,"adj":0.268},{"rate":2.634,"adj":0.268}],[{"max":5000,"rate":14.524,"adj":9.068},{"rate":13.883,"adj":9.068}]]
   - No flatdemandstructure listed

   Wait, I need to re-read the data. Let me look at the GS-4 entry again:

   ```
   "name":"GS-4 (Large General Service Primary Voltage)",
   "demandratestructure":[[{"max":5000,"rate":3.275,"adj":0.268},{"rate":2.634,"adj":0.268}],[{"max":5000,"rate":14.524,"adj":9.068},{"rate":13.883,"adj":9.068}]],
   ```

   So the on-peak demand charge for GS-4 is:
   - First 5,000 kW: $14.524 + $9.068 = $23.592/kW
   - Over 5,000 kW: $13.883 + $9.068 = $22.951/kW

   Now let me compare with GS-3:
   - GS-3 on-peak: $14.569 + $10.39 = $24.959/kW

   So GS-3 has a higher on-peak demand charge than GS-4.

   Now let me also check Schedule 10 (Large General Service):
   - Entry 6997d9ec24c59a06a0022259 (Secondary):
     - flatdemandstructure: [[{"rate":3.316,"adj":3.189}]]
     - So flat demand = $3.316 + $3.189 = $6.505/kW
     - No demandratestructure listed (it's a flat demand only)
   
   - Entry 6997dae6d3c1f5b8140d5aad (Primary):
     - flatdemandstructure: [[{"max":5000,"rate":2.717,"adj":3.469},{"rate":2.076,"adj":3.469}]]
     - First 5,000 kW: $2.717 + $3.469 = $6.186/kW
     - Over 5,000 kW: $2.076 + $3.469 = $5.545/kW

   Schedule 10 has much lower demand charges than GS-3 or GS-4.

   Now let me also check the 5P Church and Synagogue TOU Service:
   - demandratestructure: [[{"rate":0}],[{"rate":6.789}],[{"rate":9.726}]]
   - No adjustments listed
   - So max demand = $9.726/kW

   And the NC schedules (which are for North Carolina, not Virginia):
   - Small General Service TOU: demand rate up to $9.786/kW
   - These are NC schedules, not VA

   So comparing all Virginia commercial schedules:

   | Schedule | Service Class | Highest Demand Charge ($/kW) |
   |----------|--------------|------------------------------|
   | GS-1 | Small General Service (<30 kW) | $0 (no demand charge) |
   | GS-2 Demand | Intermediate (30-500 kW) | $12.785/kW (winter) |
   | GS-2 Non-Demand | Intermediate (30-500 kW) | $0 (no demand charge) |
   | GS-2T | Intermediate TOU (30-500 kW) | $16.192/kW (summer on-peak) |
   | GS-3 | Large General Service Secondary (≥500 kW) | $24.959/kW (on-peak) |
   | GS-4 | Large General Service Primary (≥500 kW) | $23.592/kW (on-peak, first 5,000 kW) |
   | Schedule 10 Secondary | Large General Service (≥500 kW) | $6.505/kW (flat) |
   | Schedule 10 Primary | Large General Service (≥500 kW) | $6.186/kW (flat) |
   | 5P | Church TOU | $9.726/kW |

   So the highest demand charge is for **Schedule GS-3 (Large General Service Secondary Voltage)** at **$24.959/kW** during on-peak periods (summer months, June-September).

   Wait, let me double-check the GS-3 demand charge. The data shows:
   - demandratestructure: [[{"rate":3.636,"adj":10.39}],[{"rate":14.569,"adj":10.39}]]
   
   So:
   - Tier 1 (off-peak): $3.636 + $10.39 = $14.026/kW
   - Tier 2 (on-peak): $14.569 + $10.39 = $24.959/kW

   Yes, GS-3 has the highest demand charge at $24.959/kW during on-peak periods.

   Let me also verify the GS-2T demand charge from the tariff document:
   - Distribution Demand: $4.963/kW
   - Generation Demand (Summer): $4.526/kW
   - Transmission Demand: $2.313/kW
   - Total base: $4.963 + $4.526 + $2.313 = $11.802/kW
   - Plus adjustments/riders: $4.39/kW (from the IURDB data)
   - Total: $11.802 + $4.39 = $16.192/kW

   So GS-3 at $24.959/kW is indeed the highest.

   Let me also check if there are any other schedules I might have missed. Looking at the search results, I see:
   - Schedule 6TS (Thermal Storage): Generation Demand Charge @ $7.335/kW + Distribution Demand up to $4.112/kW = ~$11.447/kW (before riders)
   - Schedule 8 (Supplementary, Maintenance, Standby Service) - not a standard service class
   - Schedule SCR - not in the IURDB data

   So the answer is **Schedule GS-3 (Large General Service Secondary Voltage)** with the highest demand charge of approximately **$24.96/kW** during on-peak periods.

   Let me also note that the GS-3 demand charge breakdown is:
   - Distribution demand: $3.636/kW
   - Generation/Transmission demand: $10.39/kW (adjustments/riders)
   - On-peak generation demand: $14.569/kW
   - Total on-peak: $3.636 + $14.569 + $10.39 = ... wait, that's not right.

   Actually, looking at the demandratestructure more carefully:
   - Tier 1: $3.636 + $10.39 = $14.026/kW (this is the off-peak or all-months distribution demand)
   - Tier 2: $14.569 + $10.39 = $24.959/kW (this is the on-peak demand)

   The demandcomments say: "Adjustments = Biomass Conversions + Brunswick County Power Station + Clean Energy Projects and Power Purchase Agreements + Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind + Environmental Projects + Grid Transformation + Greensville Power Station + Rural Broadband Pilot Projects + Surry and North Anna Nuclear Life Extension Program + Transmission + US Solar Projects"

   So the $10.39/kW in adjustments includes transmission and various riders.

   The total on-peak demand charge for GS-3 is $24.959/kW, which is the highest among all Dominion Energy Virginia commercial service classes.
